Fallback and Kernel Dispatch on GFX900:

GPUs like the GeForce GTX 970 or Radeon RX 480 have a dedicated GPU core that handles graphics operations, while the CPU is responsible for processing data. This means that when you run a program that requires both graphics and CPU-intensive tasks, such as video editing or gaming, the CPU will handle the graphics operations, while the GPU will handle the more computationally intensive tasks on the CPU.

Fallback: Fallback is a technique used to bypass the dedicated GPU core when it's not available. In this case, the CPU will take over the graphics processing and display the output on the monitor. This can be
